name: Palm Beach County School District
districtFact: Palm Beach County School District's Spring Break has historically aligned with Miami-Dade and Broward County Public Schools — all three South Florida systems releasing the same week — which is why hotels between Boca Raton and Jupiter typically begin filling quickly once the academic calendar is published each fall.

about.title: About Palm Beach County School District
about.content: Palm Beach County School District is the fifth-largest in Florida, serving about 190,000 students across more than 185 schools in a county that stretches roughly 80 miles from Jupiter on the northern coast to Boca Raton in the south — and another 45 miles inland to Belle Glade near Lake Okeechobee. The same district covers the oceanfront communities of Palm Beach and Manalapan, the horse country of Wellington, the tech and biotech corridor around Boca Raton, and the agricultural towns of the interior where many families work in seasonal farm operations. Most students attend their neighborhood school, with assignment based on home address. A school choice and magnet program system exists for families seeking specialized programs, but applications close in January — earlier than most newcomers expect. Charter schools operate separately and require their own enrollment process.

about.title: The School Calendar, Hurricanes, and the South Florida Season
about.content: The school year typically begins in mid-August and runs through late May. Florida requires a minimum number of instructional hours, not a fixed number of days, so the specific start and end dates shift each year based on how hurricane makeup days are structured. The district builds a bank of emergency closure days into the calendar — if the days go unused, the year ends earlier; if storms force closures, the makeup schedule extends into late May or early June. Households booking travel at the end of May should allow for this flexibility. Hurricane season officially runs June through November, but August and September are the highest-risk months for South Florida — a fact that affects school opening logistics for households arriving during the peak of storm season. The calendar pressure at the other end of the year comes from a very different source. Spring Break timing has historically aligned with Miami-Dade County Public Schools and Broward County Public Schools, with all three South Florida mega-districts releasing the same week. That concentration of demand hits Palm Beach International Airport, US-1, and coastal hotels simultaneously — planning early is less about preference than about availability.

content: In 2026-2027, Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade all share Spring Break March 22–26. Palm Beach coastal hotels (Boca Raton to Jupiter) fill fast once the tri-county overlap is announced — book early.

Palm Beach County spans from wealthy coastal towns to rural agricultural communities near Lake Okeechobee. Confirm your school assignment by address rather than assuming by city name — the district covers a very wide range of environments.
Hurricane season risk is lower than Miami-Dade but real. The district banks makeup days, but extended closures could push the last day past May 27. Book late-May travel with flexible cancellation.

School choice and magnet applications open in the fall and close earlier than most newcomers expect. If you're arriving between September and December, contact the Palm Beach County school choice office right away — don't wait for spring open house season.

districtFaqs.q: Is Palm Beach County School District the same as Palm Beach Atlantic University or FAU?
districtFaqs.a: No. Palm Beach County School District (PBCSD) is the K-12 public school system serving Palm Beach County. Palm Beach Atlantic University is a private Christian university in West Palm Beach, and Florida Atlantic University (FAU) has a major campus in Boca Raton. Both are separate institutions with their own semester academic calendars, completely independent of the K-12 school district.

districtFaqs.q: Does Palm Beach County School District serve the whole county including western farm communities?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Palm Beach County School District serves all of Palm Beach County, which spans from the Atlantic coastline to Lake Okeechobee — the largest county by land area in Florida. The western portion of the county includes agricultural communities like Belle Glade and Pahokee with a significant migrant farm worker population. The district calendar applies uniformly across the county, but the school environment and demographics vary considerably between coastal and inland communities.

districtFaqs.q: What school district is Boca Raton in?
districtFaqs.a: Boca Raton is served by Palm Beach County School District. Despite being located near the Broward County line, all Boca Raton addresses fall within Palm Beach County and are assigned to PBCSD schools. The closest Broward County districts — Broward County Public Schools — begin south of the county line.

districtFaqs.q: What school district is Wellington in?
districtFaqs.a: Wellington is within Palm Beach County and is served by Palm Beach County School District. Wellington-area students attend PBCSD campuses. The equestrian community around the Winter Equestrian Festival grounds is entirely within PBCSD boundaries.

districtFaqs.q: What school district is Jupiter in?
districtFaqs.a: Jupiter is in northern Palm Beach County and is served by Palm Beach County School District. Some addresses in the Jupiter Farms area and near the Martin County line may fall in Martin County School District — verify your specific address at palmbeachschools.org before assuming your assignment.

districtFaqs.q: When does Palm Beach County school start and end?
districtFaqs.a: The school year typically begins in mid-August and ends in late May, subject to hurricane makeup days. The specific start and end dates are set each year by the school board. In 2026–2027, the school year runs from mid-August through May 27. Check palmbeachschools.org each fall for the approved calendar, as exact dates shift year to year.

districtFaqs.q: When is Spring Break in Palm Beach County?
districtFaqs.a: Spring Break in Palm Beach County has historically fallen in late March, typically aligning with Miami-Dade County Public Schools and Broward County Public Schools. In 2026–2027, Spring Break runs March 22–26. Because all three South Florida districts break the same week, hotels on the Palm Beach coast and travel out of PBI and FLL typically see a sharp increase in bookings once the calendar is published in the fall.

districtFaqs.q: How do hurricanes affect the Palm Beach County school calendar?
districtFaqs.a: When a hurricane or tropical storm threatens the area, the district closes schools and activates its emergency notification system. The official calendar includes built-in makeup days — if those days are used for storm closures, they are made up later in the year, which can push the last day of school past the originally scheduled date. If the makeup days go unused, the year ends as scheduled or slightly earlier. Households booking travel at the end of May should verify the current makeup day status before finalizing plans.

districtFaqs.q: Does Palm Beach County School District offer magnet or school choice programs?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. PBCSD operates magnet schools and a school choice system that allows families to apply to schools outside their neighborhood attendance zone. Applications typically open in the fall and close in January — earlier than most families arriving mid-year expect. Contact the district's school choice office in the fall for available programs and timelines. Charter schools also operate within the county as independent public school options with their own enrollment processes.

districtFaqs.q: Does Palm Beach County provide school bus transportation?
districtFaqs.a: PBCSD provides bus service to students who live beyond a designated walk zone from their assigned school. Eligibility depends on distance from the campus and the student's grade level. Transportation for magnet and school choice program students follows separate rules — not all choice program attendees qualify for district transportation. Check eligibility at palmbeachschools.org after receiving your school assignment.

districtFaqs.q: How does Palm Beach County's calendar compare to Miami-Dade and Broward?
districtFaqs.a: All three South Florida districts generally align their Spring Break dates, which concentrates travel demand and resort bookings across the region simultaneously. Specific start dates, teacher planning days, and mid-year break schedules vary. Palm Beach County's calendar is set independently by its own school board — check each district's published calendar separately rather than assuming they match exactly.

districtFaqs.q: Does Palm Beach County have year-round schools?
districtFaqs.a: The majority of PBCSD schools follow a traditional calendar running from mid-August through late May. A small number of schools have historically operated on modified or year-round schedules. Check the individual school's calendar on palmbeachschools.org, as not all campuses follow the same schedule.

livingHere.intro: Palm Beach County doesn't look like one place. The coastal towns from Jupiter to Boca Raton operate on a different rhythm than the agricultural communities inland — resort season, equestrian events, and the annual return of seasonal residents shape school-year logistics in ways that don't appear on the academic calendar itself. A household in Palm Beach Gardens and one in Belle Glade technically belong to the same school district, but the school experience, commute, and local infrastructure around those schools have almost nothing in common.

highlights.label: Palm Beach International Airport (PBI)
highlights.detail: A mid-size regional airport in West Palm Beach with direct service to major US hubs. It's noticeably less congested than MIA or FLL, making it the preferred choice for many county families. Spring break week sees significant booking surges — reserve flights 6–8 weeks out.

highlights.label: Winter Resort Season
highlights.detail: Palm Beach island and the coastal towns fill with seasonal residents and their staff from November through April. Traffic on US-1 and A1A, restaurant reservations, and private club waitlists all spike during this window, which overlaps almost entirely with the school year.

highlights.label: Wellington Equestrian Season
highlights.detail: The Wellington area hosts the Winter Equestrian Festival from January through April — one of the largest and longest equestrian competitions in the world. Families in western Palm Beach County regularly navigate horse trailer traffic and show-related road congestion during the school semester.

highlights.label: Spring Training Baseball
highlights.detail: Roger Dean Chevrolet Stadium in Jupiter hosts both the Miami Marlins and St. Louis Cardinals for spring training in February and March, coinciding with the end of winter resort season. Day games on school days are common — tickets are in demand but often available with short notice.

highlights.label: Boca Raton Tech and Biotech Corridor
highlights.detail: Boca Raton is home to a significant concentration of tech, biotech, and financial services firms, including FAU's research park. Many professional families relocating to South Palm Beach County cite this employment base — traffic on I-95 and the Turnpike during school-day commutes is the main practical impact.

highlights.label: Agricultural Western Communities
highlights.detail: Belle Glade, Pahokee, and South Bay near Lake Okeechobee are part of the same school district as coastal Boca Raton. These communities serve large migrant farm worker populations, and attendance patterns in western schools can shift during the October–December harvest season.

highlights.label: Hurricane Season and School Closures
highlights.detail: August and September bring the highest hurricane risk to South Florida, overlapping with the first weeks of school. When a storm threatens, the district closes schools — sometimes for multiple days — and activates its makeup-day schedule, which can push the last day of school later than the original calendar shows. The district announces closures through its emergency notification system and the palmbeachschools.org website. For households that moved from inland states where weather rarely affects the school calendar, the first major storm warning of the year is often the steepest adjustment: school closes, daycares and aftercare programs follow, and the week's schedule disappears on short notice. A backup childcare plan and several days of emergency supplies are standard practice for families who've been through a South Florida hurricane season before.

relatedDistricts.name: Miami-Dade County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Florida's largest school district and Palm Beach County's closest major neighbor to the south. Miami-Dade and Palm Beach County have historically shared Spring Break timing, meaning families comparing the two districts will see similar break windows but different school start dates and instructional calendars.

relatedDistricts.name: Broward County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: Located between Palm Beach County and Miami-Dade, Broward County Public Schools is the second-largest district in Florida. Broward and Palm Beach typically align on Spring Break, and families relocating along the South Florida coast — particularly around Boca Raton and Deerfield Beach near the county line — often compare the two districts.

relatedDistricts.name: Orange County Public Schools
relatedDistricts.reason: A large Central Florida district that serves the Orlando metro area. Useful for families comparing Florida school calendar structures or relocating between South Florida and the Orlando corridor.
